data.title: Airbnb gains after Wells Fargo turns bullish ahead of earnings
data.description:
Airbnb (ABNB) rallied in early action on Wednesday after Wells Fargo upgraded the online travel stock to an Overweight rating after having it set at Equal-weight. 

Analyst Ken Gawrelski and his team think Airbnb (ABNB) is at a significant business inflection point after emerging from two years of decelerating growth and compressing margins to head into a period of accelerating growth and expanding margins for 2026-2028.

"In our view, we see new growth initiatives, such as hotels and sponsored listings, as accretive to financials, contributing to accelerating room nights growth and margin expansion," highlighted Gawrelski.

Notably, the firm expects Airbnb (ABNB) to double its OTA hotel share to 8% in 2028 from 4% currently in its core five markets. In particular, stronger merchandising in New York City and AI search are seen as helping to unlock hotel supply.

Wells Fargo raised its price target on Airbnb (ABNB) to $178 from $136, based on a 25X multiple to the 2028 EPS estimate.

Shares of Airbnb (ABNB) were up 2.3% in premarket trading to $145.91 vs. the 52-week range of $110.81 to $145.77. Airbnb (ABNB) is lined up to report first-quarter earnings on May 7.

data.title: Lifezone Metals Limited prices $25M share sale
data.description:
• Lifezone Metals (LZM) will sell 5.7M shares at $4.40 each to institutional investors.
  • The offering is expected to generate ~$25M in gross proceeds.
  • Funds will support exploration activities in Burundi and Tanzania.
  • Proceeds will also be used for the PGM Recycling Project and hydrometallurgical R&D at Simulus Laboratory.
  • Additional uses include general corporate purposes, working capital, and financing costs.
  • The transaction is expected to close around April 23, 2026.
  • LZM shares down 5% premarket.

data.title: Preferred Bank GAAP EPS of $2.53 beats by $0.07, revenue of $69.62M misses by $0.18M
data.description:
• Preferred Bank press release (PFBC): Q1 GAAP EPS of $2.53 beats by $0.07.
  • Revenue of $69.62M (+4.5% Y/Y) misses by $0.18M.
  • Return on average assets was 1.67%
  • Return on average equity was 16.00%
  • Total loans increased by $68.6 million or 1.1%, linked quarter
  • Total deposits increased by $74.7 million, or 1.2%, linked quarter
  • The efficiency ratio for the quarter was 33.8%.
  • The provision for credit losses for the first quarter of 2026 was $1.5 million compared to $4.3 million in the prior quarter and compared to $700,000 in the same quarter last year.
  • The Bank’s allowance coverage ratio was 1.24% of total loans held for investment compared to 1.30% last quarter and compared to 1.28% in the first quarter of 2025.

data.title: Accenture and Google Cloud expand partnership to scale AI transformation with Gemini Enterprise
data.description:
• Accenture (ACN) and Google Cloud (GOOG) (GOOGL) Wednesday announced a significant expansion of their partnership, with the launch of a first-of-its-kind program focused on helping enterprises drive AI-powered reinvention and business value faster and at scale.
  • The Gemini Enterprise Acceleration Program combines Google Cloud and Accenture AI teams to speed up execution.

data.title: Asian fleets seen returning first to Hormuz as Western shippers stay cautious
data.description:
Asian shipowners may be among the first to resume voyages through the Strait of Hormuz following a fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Many are viewed as more willing to accept the risks and costs that continue to deter Western operators, Reuters reported Wednesday, citing shipping executives at a conference.

Traffic through the critical waterway has been severely disrupted since late February, leaving hundreds of tankers and cargo vessels stranded inside the Gulf and snarling global flows of crude oil and liquefied natural gas in what industry participants described as an unprecedented energy shipping shock.

Executives speaking at the FT Commodities Global Summit said vessels tied to countries such as India and China could gain earlier access because of government relationships, naval protection or a greater readiness to navigate sanctions and security concerns.

Larry Johnson, global head of freight at Mercuria, said state-backed convoys from countries such as India or China could potentially move through if political channels support them. Ships that have managed to transit recently were largely government-owned or otherwise backed by direct state-to-state communication with Tehran, advantages that many commercial operators lack, he said.

Peter Weernink, chief executive of SwissMarine, said fleets from India, Iraq and China may return before European or U.S.-linked companies.

Tolls and security risks remain

Iran has also tightened control over the strait by seeking toll payments in coordination with the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps, according to executives at the event, adding another layer of cost and complexity for shipowners.

Andrew Jamieson, co-head of Gunvor’s shipping arm Clearlake, said traffic could remain subdued because insurance premiums are expected to rise and crews may refuse assignments they view as dangerous.

Shipping fleet could pivot quickly

Roger Horton, chief commercial officer at Clarkson (CKNHF) (CKNHY), said the global tanker fleet could respond rapidly if the route reopens in a sustained way, even after many ships shifted to Atlantic Basin trades during the crisis to capture elevated freight rates.

A durable reopening of Hormuz would likely reshape tanker routes, freight pricing and the near-term outlook for global energy supply chains.

data.title: KeyCorp to acquire UK-based Clearwater Corporate Finance, terms undisclosed
data.description:
KeyCorp (KEY) is set to acquire the UK-based Clearwater Corporate Finance, marking the diversified bank's entry into the Western European market.

The acquisition is expected to expand KeyCorp's ability to provide financial advisory services to institutional clients.

Financial terms of the transaction were not disclosed.

The transaction is expected to close in the second half of 2026.

data.title: Regal Rexnord taps Schneider Electric’s Aamir Paul as CEO
data.description:
Regal Rexnord (RRX) has appointed Aamir Paul as chief executive officer, effective no later than July 1, 2026, following the completion of his responsibilities at Schneider Electric SE (SBGSF) (SBGSY).

Paul joined Schneider Electric in 2013 and, during his tenure, held multiple senior leadership roles of increasing responsibility across sales, business operations, and commercial execution. Prior to Schneider Electric, he spent over 13 years at Dell Technologies.

The board also named Paul to serve as a member of the board of directors effective upon the start of his employment, with an initial term running through the company’s 2027 annual meeting of shareholders.

As previously disclosed on October 29, 2025, the board initiated a comprehensive search to identify a successor to current CEO, Louis Pinkham.

Pinkham will remain CEO until Paul's start date. He will also resign from the board effective on his last day as CEO.

Source: Press Release

data.title: SoundHound AI's LivePerson deal to expand customers, better position company: Wedbush
data.description:
Wedbush said SoundHound AI's (SOUN) planned acquisition of LivePerson (LPSN) will better position the company and broaden its customer portfolio.

The firm maintained its Outperform rating and $12 price target on SoundHound's stock. Shares of the voice AI solutions provider climbed about 4% premarket on Wednesday.

On Tuesday, SoundHound said it was going to acquire voice and conversational AI company LivePerson for $43M.

"The company continues to leverage an extensive M&A strategy to further enhance its voice AI capabilities that integrate into its proprietary tech stack while expanding its customer reach by adding more businesses to cross-sell/upsell into. We believe this was a strategic move by SOUN that will better position the company to meet this transformational market shift coming while broadening its customer portfolio," said analysts led by Dan Ives.

The analysts noted that LivePerson has become an enterprise leader in predictable conversational AI, powering about 1B messages monthly, and providing rich data analytics, agent training, and AI evaluation tools to unlock the power of conversational AI for better business outcomes.

The analysts added that the acquisition marks SoundHound's expansion into AI-driven digital customer service, unifying the company's voice and agentic AI platform with LivePerson’s digital engagement capabilities to deliver additional revenue and scale while enhancing the company’s position as a provider of voice and agentic AI to premier enterprise businesses.

SoundHound had said on Tuesday that by offering its voice AI to LivePerson's customers and the unified digital and voice omnichannel solution to SoundHound's customers, the combined business is expected to reach $500M, based on the existing customer base alone.

data.title: TE Connectivity sinks after mixed Q2 results
data.description:
TE Connectivity (TEL) shares sank nearly 6% in premarket trading on Wednesday as the electrical components company reported fiscal second-quarter results that were mixed compared to Wall Street's estimates.

For the period ending March 27, TE Connectivity said it earned an adjusted $2.73  per share, as revenue rose 14.5% year-over-year to come in at $4.74B. Analysts had expected the company to earn $2.70 per share on an adjusted basis, and generate $4.76B in revenue. 

"Our teams delivered another quarter of results above guidance, including double-digit sales growth and record adjusted EPS," said CEO Terrence Curtin, in a statement. "This performance and our record orders were driven by our strategic positioning in key trends including AI, next generation transportation and electric grid modernization, along with the broadening of growth across our portfolio. We're well positioned to capitalize on the proliferation of data and power to provide our customers with leading interconnect technologies. Our strong margin performance reflects the resilience we've built to mitigate the dynamic environment we continue to operate in around the world."

For the third-quarter, TE Connectivity expects sales of approximately $5B, up roughly 10% year-over-year. Adjusted earnings per share are expected to be approximately $2.83, above the $2.79 consensus.

data.title: Boeing's loss narrows as revenue tops estimates
data.description:
Boeing (BA) rose 3.5% in premarket trading Wednesday after reporting quarterly results that topped Wall Street expectations on revenue and adjusted earnings, as investors weighed improving aircraft deliveries against ongoing geopolitical and travel-market risks.

The planemaker reported first-quarter revenue of $22.22 billion, up 14% from a year earlier and slightly above the Wall Street consensus estimate of $21.91 billion. Adjusted loss was $0.20 a share, better than analyst expectations for a loss of $0.67 a share.

Net loss fell to $7 million, or $0.11 a share, from $31 million, or $0.16 a share, a year earlier.

Before Wednesday, Boeing (BA) shares had climbed 38% over the prior 12 months as investors grew more optimistic that the aerospace giant was regaining operational footing.

The company has worked to overcome safety issues, production scrutiny and supply-chain constraints while airlines worldwide race to secure new aircraft amid strong travel demand. Markets are also looking for management commentary on whether the war in Iran could disrupt deliveries, supply routes or airline spending and whether rising jet fuel prices might pressure carriers and future travel demand.

Cash burn improves

Boeing (BA) reported operating cash flow of negative $179 million, improved from negative $1.62 billion a year earlier. Free cash flow was negative $1.45 billion, also better than the prior-year period.

CEO highlights safety, quality, backlog

“We're building on our momentum with a strong start to the year and growing record-breaking backlog across our business,” Chief Executive Kelly Ortberg said in the earnings release.

He added that Boeing (BA) remains focused on safety and quality while increasing production to meet customer commitments.

Commercial deliveries rise

Boeing (BA) said commercial airplane deliveries increased to 143 in the quarter from 130 a year earlier, helping lift revenue in its largest business segment.

Commercial airplanes revenue rose 13% to $9.2 billion, while the unit’s operating loss widened slightly to $563 million. Boeing (BA) said the 737 program continues producing at a rate of 42 aircraft a month.

The company said it still expects certification of the 737-7 and 737-10 variants in 2026, with first deliveries anticipated in 2027. The 777X program also continued certification progress, with first delivery expected in 2027.

Record backlog supports outlook

Total company backlog rose to a record $695 billion, including more than 6,100 commercial airplanes. Commercial aircraft backlog alone stood at $576 billion.

That order book gives Boeing (BA) long-term production visibility, though investors remain focused on whether the company can steadily raise output and convert demand into cash flow.
